summary: 2D web-based shooter game called 'Dark KillZone' with HTML5 Canvas gameplay featuring player movement, shooting mechanics, enemy spawning, and collision detection
architectural_notes: Vanilla JavaScript game loop using requestAnimationFrame
Simple collision detection using bounding box algorithm
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Dark atmospheric hero background image with combat/battle theme for landing page (hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)

existing_features.title: Create a landing page with a hero section featuring the title 'ENTER THE BATTLE', a 'START GAME' button, and a dark radial gradient background transitioning from #111 to #000
Implement a navigation bar with 'Dark KillZone' branding in red (#ff003c) with glow effect, semi-transparent dark background with backdrop blur
Build a 2D canvas-based shooter game with HTML5 Canvas (500x400px) that displays when the START GAME button is clicked, hiding the landing page
Implement player character as a red square (30x30px) positioned at the bottom center of the canvas, controllable with left/right arrow keys moving 20px per press
Implement shooting mechanics where spacebar fires yellow bullets (5x10px) upward from the player position at 5px per frame
Implement enemy spawning system that creates white square enemies (30x30px) at random x positions at the top of the canvas every 1 second, moving downward at 2px per frame
Implement collision detection between bullets and enemies that destroys both objects on contact using rectangular bounding box detection
Implement game loop using requestAnimationFrame that continuously clears the canvas and redraws all game entities (player, bullets, enemies)
Apply consistent dark theme styling with red accent color (#ff003c), including glow effects on interactive elements and text shadows
